content: This project entails the detailing, fabrication, and erection of a 180-ton, three-story structural steel frame. The building's lateral stability is achieved through a moment-resisting frame system, providing significant architectural freedom by eliminating the need for diagonal bracing. All connections are designed to be fully rigid or semi-rigid, with design, materials, and workmanship specified in accordance with AISC 360-22. The completed steel structure will serve as the primary load-bearing system for a commercial or institutional facility.

Total Steel Tonnage: 180 tons Total Member Pieces (Est.): 350 - 450 pieces Total Connections (Est.): 900 - 1,100 Primary Moment Connections (Est.): 150 - 200 locations (field welded or fully-tensioned bolted) Primary Material: ASTM A992 (W-shapes), A572 Gr. 50 (plates)

Complex Fabrication & Weld Quality: Moment connections demand high-precision fabrication and stringent weld procedures (e.g., CJP welds). Failure to meet tolerances or NDT requirements will lead to costly rework and schedule delays. Field Fit-Up & Erection Tolerances: The rigid nature of the frame makes erection highly sensitive to fabrication accuracy and column plumbness. Minor deviations can compound, preventing proper connection alignment and requiring significant field remediation. Skilled Labor Availability: Erection and welding of moment connections require a highly skilled workforce (certified welders, experienced ironworkers). A shortage of qualified labor in the project region could impact schedule and quality. Incomplete Connection Design: Ambiguities or delays in receiving fully detailed connection designs from the EOR will halt fabrication progress and create a cascade of RFIs, impacting the entire project timeline. Material Price Volatility & Lead Times: Unforeseen fluctuations in steel prices or extended mill lead times for specific sections can jeopardize budget adherence and the procurement schedule.

Schedule Acceleration via Prefabrication: With a locked-in design, the entire frame can be fabricated off-site in a controlled environment while foundation work proceeds, enabling rapid erection and compression of the overall construction schedule. Architectural Flexibility: The moment frame system provides open, unobstructed floor plans, offering maximum flexibility for interior space planning and future modifications, which is a key value proposition for the building owner. Value Engineering of Connection Types: A thorough review with the EOR could identify locations where semi-rigid connections or partial-strength connections are sufficient, potentially reducing fabrication complexity and cost without compromising performance. BIM Integration for Accuracy: Leveraging a fully integrated BIM model from design through fabrication (LOD 400) will mitigate clashes with MEP/façade systems and ensure precise fabrication, minimizing field errors and rework. High Structural Performance: The inherent ductility and redundancy of a moment frame offers superior seismic performance and structural robustness, resulting in a safer, more resilient building with a long service life.
Based on project scope and complexity, a 16 to 20-week schedule is recommended from notice to proceed (NTP) to completion of steel erection.
Detailing & Approval: 4 - 6 weeks Material Procurement & Fabrication: 8 - 10 weeks (runs partially concurrent with detailing) Field Erection & Final Weld/Bolt-Up: 4 - 6 weeks
